examples.title: Merge Sort Implementation
examples.description: Classic Divide and Conquer sorting algorithm
examples.explanation: Divide array at midpoint, recursively sort both halves, merge sorted halves using two-pointer technique

examples.title: Quick Sort Implementation
examples.description: In-place Divide and Conquer sorting algorithm
examples.explanation: Partition array around pivot (last element), elements smaller than pivot go left, larger go right. Recursively sort partitions.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) average, O(n²) worst

examples.title: Binary Search Implementation
examples.description: Divide and Conquer search in sorted array
examples.explanation: At each step, compare target with middle element and search in appropriate half. Reduces search space by half each time.

examples.title: Maximum and Minimum using Divide and Conquer
examples.description: Find both max and min elements in one pass
examples.code:
def find_max_min(arr, low, high):
    # Base case: single element
    if low == high:
        return arr[low], arr[low]
    # Base case: two elements
    if high == low + 1:
        if arr[low] > arr[high]:
            return arr[low], arr[high]
        else:
            return arr[high], arr[low]
    
    # Divide
    mid = (low + high) // 2
    max1, min1 = find_max_min(arr, low, mid)
    max2, min2 = find_max_min(arr, mid + 1, high)
    
    # Conquer and Combine
    overall_max = max(max1, max2)
    overall_min = min(min1, min2)
    
    return overall_max, overall_min

# Example: arr = [3, 5, 1, 9, 7, 2, 8, 4]
# Result: max=9, min=1
examples.explanation: Recursively find max/min in left and right halves, then compare results. Uses 3 comparisons at combine step (2 for max/min comparison, 1 for deciding which max/min is overall).
